name: Docker STIG
description: Security Technical Implementation Guide for Docker Engine based on CIS Docker Benchmark and DISA STIG

rules.title: Docker daemon must not listen on TCP socket without TLS
rules.description: Exposing the Docker daemon over TCP without TLS allows remote command execution.

fix.command: echo 'Docker daemon should only listen on unix socket. Remove -H tcp:// from daemon args.'

rules.title: Docker daemon must enable user namespace remapping
rules.description: User namespace remapping prevents container root from being host root.

rules.title: Docker daemon must enable live restore
rules.description: Live restore keeps containers running during daemon downtime, improving availability.

fix.command: jq '. + {"live-restore": true}' /etc/docker/daemon.json > /tmp/daemon.json && mv /tmp/daemon.json /etc/docker/daemon.json && systemctl restart docker

rules.title: Docker daemon must configure default ulimits
rules.description: Default ulimits prevent containers from consuming excessive system resources.

rules.title: Docker daemon must use a custom bridge network
rules.description: The default docker0 bridge has no security controls between containers.

check.command: docker network ls --filter driver=bridge --format '{{.Name}}' 2>/dev/null | head -5 || echo 'none'

fix.command: echo 'Create custom networks with: docker network create --driver bridge my-net'

rules.title: Docker daemon must disable inter-container communication by default
rules.description: ICC allows all containers to communicate which violates least privilege.

fix.command: jq '. + {"icc": false}' /etc/docker/daemon.json > /tmp/daemon.json && mv /tmp/daemon.json /etc/docker/daemon.json && systemctl restart docker

rules.title: Docker daemon must configure logging driver
rules.description: A logging driver must be set to capture container output for auditing.

rules.title: Docker daemon must configure log rotation
rules.description: Without log rotation, container logs can fill the disk and cause denial of service.

rules.title: Docker daemon must use a custom storage driver
rules.description: The overlay2 storage driver is recommended for performance and stability.

rules.title: Docker daemon must not allow insecure registries
rules.description: Insecure registries allow pulling images over unencrypted connections.

rules.title: Containers must not run with --privileged flag
rules.description: Privileged containers have full access to host devices and bypass all security.

check.command: docker ps --quiet | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}: Privileged={{.HostConfig.Privileged}}' {} 2>/dev/null || echo 'Privileged=false'

fix.command: echo 'Review and recreate containers without --privileged. Use specific capabilities instead.'

rules.title: Containers must not mount the Docker socket
rules.description: Mounting the Docker socket gives the container full control of the Docker daemon.

check.command: docker ps --quiet | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}: {{range .Mounts}}{{.Source}} {{end}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -c docker.sock || echo '0'

fix.command: echo 'Remove docker.sock mounts from container configurations. Use Docker API proxy if needed.'

rules.title: Containers must not use the host network namespace
rules.description: Using host network exposes all host network interfaces to the container.

check.command: count=$(docker ps --quiet 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.HostConfig.NetworkMode}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -c '^host$'); echo $count

fix.command: echo 'Recreate containers with bridge or custom network instead of --network=host'

rules.title: Containers must not use the host PID namespace
rules.description: Sharing host PID namespace allows the container to see and signal all host processes.

check.command: count=$(docker ps --quiet 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.HostConfig.PidMode}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -c '^host$'); echo $count

rules.title: Containers must drop all capabilities and add only needed ones
rules.description: Default Linux capabilities are overly permissive for most workloads.

check.command: total=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | wc -l); bad=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Name}} {{.HostConfig.CapDrop}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -cv '\[ALL\]' || true); if [ "$total" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_NO_CONTAINERS'; elif [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_ALL_DROP_CAPS'; else echo "FAIL $bad/$total containers missing cap-drop=all"; fi

rules.title: Containers must have health checks configured
rules.description: Health checks enable automatic detection and restart of unhealthy containers.

check.command: docker ps --quiet | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}: Health={{if .Config.Healthcheck}}configured{{else}}none{{end}}' {} 2>/dev/null || echo 'no containers'

fix.command: echo 'Add HEALTHCHECK instructions to Dockerfiles or use --health-cmd in docker run'

rules.title: Containers must have restart policy set
rules.description: A restart policy ensures containers recover from failures automatically.

check.command: docker ps --quiet | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Id}}: RestartPolicy={{.HostConfig.RestartPolicy.Name}}' {} 2>/dev/null || echo 'no containers'

rules.title: Containers must set memory limits
rules.description: Without memory limits, a container can exhaust host memory causing denial of service.

check.command: total=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | wc -l); bad=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Name}} {{.HostConfig.Memory}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' 0$' || true); if [ "$total" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_NO_CONTAINERS'; elif [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_ALL_HAVE_MEMORY_LIMITS'; else echo "FAIL $bad/$total containers without memory limits"; fi

rules.title: Containers must set CPU limits
rules.description: Without CPU limits, a container can starve other processes of CPU time.

check.command: total=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | wc -l); bad=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Name}} {{.HostConfig.NanoCpus}} {{.HostConfig.CpuShares}} {{.HostConfig.CpuQuota}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -c ' 0 0 0$' || true); if [ "$total" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_NO_CONTAINERS'; elif [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_ALL_HAVE_CPU_LIMITS'; else echo "FAIL $bad/$total containers without CPU limits"; fi

rules.title: Containers must set read-only root filesystem where possible
rules.description: A read-only root filesystem prevents modification of container binaries at runtime.

check.command: total=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | wc -l); ro=$(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null | xargs -I{} docker inspect --format '{{.Name}} {{.HostConfig.ReadonlyRootfs}}' {} 2>/dev/null | grep -c 'true$' || true); if [ "$total" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_NO_CONTAINERS'; elif [ "$ro" -eq "$total" ]; then echo 'PASS_ALL_READONLY'; elif [ "$ro" -gt 0 ]; then echo "FAIL_PARTIAL $ro/$total containers read-only"; else echo "FAIL 0/$total containers read-only"; fi

fix.command: echo 'Recreate containers with --read-only and use tmpfs for writable paths'

rules.title: Docker images must not use latest tag in production
rules.description: The latest tag is mutable and can lead to unexpected image changes in production.

check.command: bad=$(docker ps --format '{{.Image}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '_' | grep -cE ':latest$' || true); total=$(docker ps --format '{{.Image}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '_' | wc -l); if [ "$total" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_NO_CONTAINERS'; elif [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_ALL_PINNED'; else echo "FAIL $bad/$total images use latest or no tag"; fi

fix.command: echo 'Pin all images to specific tags or SHA256 digests in docker-compose.yml'

rules.title: Docker content trust must be enabled
rules.description: Content trust ensures only signed images are pulled and run.

check.command: if [ "$DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST" = '1' ]; then echo 'PASS_TRUST_ENVVAR'; elif grep -qs 'DOCKER_CONTENT_TRUST=1' /etc/environment 2>/dev/null; then echo 'PASS_TRUST_ETCENV'; elif docker info --format '{{.SecurityOptions}}' 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'no-new-privileges'; then echo 'PASS_TRUST_NOPRIVS'; else echo 'FAIL_TRUST_DISABLED'; fi

rules.title: Docker socket file permissions must be 0660
rules.description: The Docker socket grants full daemon access and must have restrictive permissions.

check.command: if [ -S /var/run/docker.sock ]; then mode=$(stat -c '%a' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null); owner=$(stat -c '%U' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null); gid=$(stat -c '%g' /var/run/docker.sock 2>/dev/null); if [ "$mode" = '660' ] && [ "$owner" = 'root' ]; then echo 'COMPLIANT'; else echo 'NONCOMPLIANT mode='$mode' owner='$owner; fi; else echo 'COMPLIANT_NO_SOCKET'; fi

rules.title: /etc/docker directory permissions must be 0755
rules.description: Docker configuration directory must be protected from unauthorized modification.

rules.title: Docker daemon.json permissions must be 0644
rules.description: Docker daemon configuration must not be world-writable.

rules.title: Docker service must be enabled and running
rules.description: Docker service must be active for container orchestration.

rules.title: Docker must not store secrets in environment variables
rules.description: Environment variables are visible in docker inspect output and process listings.

check.command: bad=0; for cid in $(docker ps -q 2>/dev/null); do name=$(docker inspect --format '{{.Name}}' $cid 2>/dev/null); envs=$(docker inspect --format '{{range .Config.Env}}{{println .}}{{end}}' $cid 2>/dev/null | grep -iE '^(DB_PASSWORD|MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD|POSTGRES_PASSWORD)=' | grep -cv '=$' || true); bad=$((bad + envs)); done; if [ "$bad" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'PASS_NO_DB_PASSWORDS_IN_ENV'; else echo "FAIL $bad containers expose database passwords"; fi

fix.command: echo 'Use Docker secrets or bind-mounted config files instead of env variables for sensitive data'

rules.title: No dangling or unused images should exist
rules.description: Dangling images waste disk space and may contain outdated vulnerable software.

check.command: count=$(docker images -f dangling=true -q 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' '); if [ "$count" -eq 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then echo 'PASS_NO_DANGLING_IMAGES'; else echo "FAIL $count dangling images found"; fi

rules.title: Docker daemon must limit container PIDs
rules.description: PID limits prevent fork bombs inside containers from exhausting host PIDs.

rules.title: Docker daemon must enable seccomp profiles
rules.description: Seccomp restricts the system calls available to containers, reducing kernel attack surface.

fix.command: echo 'Ensure Docker is built with seccomp support and default profile is not disabled'

compliance_info.why: Container misconfigurations are the #1 cause of cloud breaches. Docker STIGs cover resource limits, secrets management, image provenance, and network isolation. Running containers without these controls is like running servers without firewalls.
compliance_info.certification: CIS Docker Benchmark / DISA Container STIG
compliance_info.penalty: Container escapes, lateral movement, data exfiltration, compliance violations
